extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property requires that flipping dyspnea from false to true (other inputs unchanged) never lowers acuity. Dyspnea appears in only one rule: interaction_drop (priority 100) IF (stiff_neck and rash and focal_weakness and dyspnea) THEN acuity := self_care. This rule has the highest priority, so when it fires it overrides everything.

Consider the base input: stiff_neck=true, rash=true, focal_weakness=true, hemoptysis=false, fever=false, back_pain=false.

Before (dyspnea=false): interaction_drop does NOT fire (dyspnea missing). Matching rules: raise_rash (priority 3 -> emergent), raise_stiff_neck (priority 2 -> urgent), raise_focal_weakness (priority 2 -> urgent). Highest priority is raise_rash at priority 3, so acuity = emergent (highest level).

After (dyspnea=true, everything else identical): interaction_drop now fires because stiff_neck and rash and focal_weakness and dyspnea are all true. At priority 100 it beats raise_rash (priority 3), so acuity = self_care (lowest level).

Setting dyspnea true drops acuity from emergent to self_care, a decrease across the full enum range. The property is violated. The witness assignment is the post-flip state (dyspnea=true) producing the lowered self_care acuity.
